action.reasoning: The task requires producing a 4-chapter technical field guide with research, drafting, editing, a glossary, and assembly. This is too large for a single EXECUTE action. I will decompose it into parallel chapter subtasks (each handling research, draft, and edit for one chapter), a glossary subtask, and take the final assembly role myself. Each chapter subtask will produce its own chapter content. The glossary will be built in parallel. I will then assemble everything into the final guide.

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 1: Introduction to LLM Agent Memory Systems
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 1, which introduces the concept of memory in LLM agents, defines key terminology, and outlines the landscape of memory architectures. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 1 on introduction to LLM agent memory systems

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 2: Short-Term and Working Memory Mechanisms
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 2, covering short-term memory, context windows, attention mechanisms, and working memory patterns in LLM agents. It must include original research, a full draft, and a consistency edit. The output will be used in the final assembly.

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 2 on short-term and working memory mechanisms

subtasks.goal: Research, draft, and edit Chapter 3: Long-Term Memory Architectures
subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 3, covering long-term memory systems including vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory stores, and retrieval-augmented generation patterns. outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 3 on long-term memory architectures

subtasks.capsule: This subtask produces Chapter 4, covering advanced topics such as memory consolidation, multi-agent memory sharing, memory debugging, and emerging research directions. self_role.goal: Assemble the final field guide from all chapter drafts and the glossary, ensuring structural coherence and adding front matter

action.reasoning: Chapter 3 is a substantial technical writing task covering vector databases, knowledge graphs, episodic memory, and RAG patterns. It requires research, drafting, and editing. To manage scope and ensure quality, I will decompose into parallel research subtasks for each major topic, then handle drafting and editing myself as the integrator. This ensures parallel discovery while maintaining a single coherent voice in the final draft.

subtasks.goal: Research vector database architectures for LLM long-term memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate vector databases (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus) used for semantic memory in LLM agents. Cover indexing strategies (HNSW, IVF), embedding models, similarity search, and scalability trade-offs. Output a structured research summary with key citations and architectural diagrams described in text.

outputs.description: Research summary on vector database architectures for LLM memory

subtasks.goal: Research knowledge graph integration for LLM long-term memory
subtasks.capsule: Investigate how knowledge graphs (e.g., Neo4j, RDF stores) are used to store structured, relational memory for LLM agents. Cover graph construction from text, query languages (Cypher, SPARQL), reasoning over graphs, and hybrid vector-graph retrieval. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on knowledge graph integration for LLM memory

subtasks.goal: Research episodic memory stores and RAG patterns for LLM agents
subtasks.capsule: Investigate episodic memory architectures (time-stamped experience stores, replay mechanisms) and retrieval-augmented generation patterns (naive RAG, modular RAG, advanced RAG with re-ranking and routing). Cover trade-offs between recall fidelity, latency, and context window constraints. Output a structured research summary.

outputs.description: Research summary on episodic memory stores and RAG patterns

outputs.description: Final edited draft of Chapter 3 on long-term memory architectures

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on memory consolidation and distillation patterns
subtasks.capsule: Cover techniques for consolidating episodic memory into semantic knowledge, including summarization, clustering, and distillation methods. Discuss trade-offs between retention fidelity and storage efficiency. This forms the first major section of Chapter 4.

subtasks.goal: Research and draft section on multi-agent memory sharing and coordination
